findings.title: POI 'always anchor' categories (Medical/Pets/Schools/Sports/Transit/Traffic) ignore useAnchorAlways at runtime — pins follow the day pin and Settings lies
findings.rootCause: shouldForceAnchor() reads ONLY STATE.preferences?.poiAnchoring?.[cat.key] === 'anchor' and never falls back to cat.useAnchorAlways. The documented contract (state.ts:94-99 and types.d.ts:634-636: 'Empty / missing keys fall back to the category's useAnchorAlways flag') was lost in the React migration — the comment still points at the old pages/home.js. Because the default is {}, shouldForceAnchor returns false for all six categories, so (a) resolveSearchCenter uses the selected day instead of the anchor, and (b) the onSelectedDayChange skip-guard at HeroMap.tsx:624 (`if (cat && shouldForceAnchor(cat)) return;`) never triggers, so they needlessly re-fetch on day change. Settings.tsx:596-601 computes effectiveAnchor WITH the useAnchorAlways fallback, so the UI and the runtime disagree.

findings.title: Adding/removing a place to the to-do list from a map InfoWindow does not repaint to-do markers — map diverges from saved state until remount
findings.rootCause: The InfoWindow toggle handler (wireInfoWindowMarkButtons.todoBtn.onclick, HeroMap.tsx:320-331) calls toggleTodoListMembership + emit('state:changed') + upsertTrip + refresh() but never calls repaintTodoMarkers(). HeroMap's map-setup useEffect depends only on [mapRetryTick], so emit('state:changed') re-renders React but does NOT re-run the effect, so no marker repaint happens. repaintTodoMarkers() is only invoked on initial mount (line 662) and inside onSelectedDayChange (line 620).

findings.title: Map InfoWindows + search dropdown are hardcoded English — every place bubble and the search UI ignore the active locale
findings.rootCause: These strings are written as literal English in the imperative innerHTML builders (HeroMap.tsx buildInfoWindowHtml, mapSearch.ts renderPredictions/_FALLBACK_CAT.label, todoMarkers.ts openTodoInfoWindow, dayMarkers.ts openDayPinInfoWindow) and in MapSearchBar.tsx's static JSX, rather than going through t(). The surrounding surface (e.g. home.searchMapPlaceholder, poiTooltips.*) IS localized, confirming this is an oversight not a deliberate degraded path.

findings.title: Map search dropdown has no combobox/listbox semantics and no keyboard navigation — unusable for keyboard/screen-reader users
findings.rootCause: MapSearchBar.tsx renders a plain <input> (no role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-autocomplete) and an empty results <div> (no role=listbox). mapSearch.ts renderPredictions emits <button class=map-search-row> rows with no role=option, no aria-selected, no id/aria-activedescendant wiring, and there is no keydown handler anywhere (grep for keydown/ArrowDown/Enter returns nothing). Selection is click-only.

findings.title: Selecting a search result that fails getDetails is a silent dead-end — no marker, no error, dropdown already closed
findings.rootCause: fetchDetails (mapSearch.ts:175-182) calls svc.getDetails and, on any status !== OK or null place, just `return`s with no user feedback. The click handler (line 224-232) has already called hideResults() and set the input value before fetchDetails runs, so there's no remaining UI cue. Contrast with renderPredictions which at least shows 'No matches.' for ZERO_RESULTS.

findings.title: Autocomplete predictions can render out of order (stale response overwrites newer) — no request-sequence guard
findings.rootCause: The 220ms debounce (typingTimer) limits request frequency but does not serialize responses. getPlacePredictions callbacks (line 210) call renderPredictions(preds) with no token/sequence check, so a slower earlier response that lands after a faster later one wins. There is no 'latest request id' comparison and no comparison of the response against the current searchInput.value.

findings.title: Places result on the to-do list double-pins on the map — POI/search pin and to-do pin overlap because skipPlaceIds is never passed
findings.rootCause: paintTodoMarkers supports a skipPlaceIds Set (documented at todoMarkers.ts:44-46, applied at line 188-189 'so the map doesn't double-pin'), but the only caller, repaintTodoMarkers in HeroMap.tsx:654-660, never passes skipPlaceIds. The set of currently-painted POI/search place_ids (available from placesMarkers / searchMarker) is never collected and forwarded.

findings.title: Map search bar offers an interactive search box that silently does nothing when the Maps API key is missing/blocked
findings.rootCause: If GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is unset, index.html:134 loads the Maps JS with an empty key, so google.maps never initializes and whenGoogleMapsReady() rejects. wireMapSearchBanner early-returns at mapSearch.ts:67 (no AutocompleteService), so NONE of the input/clear/results listeners are attached, but MapSearchBar.tsx unconditionally renders the input strip. There is no disabled/placeholder-changed/hidden state for the Maps-down case.

findings.title: wireMapSearchBanner leaks a document-level click listener on every HeroMap mount (never removed in cleanup)
findings.rootCause: mapSearch.ts:246 attaches document.addEventListener('click', …) but wireMapSearchBanner returns void and provides no teardown. HeroMap's effect cleanup (HeroMap.tsx:852-865) only removes the poiTogglesEl listener; it has no reference to remove the mapSearch document listener. The input/results/clear listeners are on React-owned nodes that get GC'd, but the document-level one persists.

findings.title: Map search + POI use deprecated legacy Places API (AutocompleteService / PlacesService) — future breakage risk
findings.rootCause: mapSearch.ts uses new google.maps.places.AutocompleteService() + svc.getDetails; HeroMap.tsx uses new google.maps.places.PlacesService(map) + svc.nearbySearch with RankBy.DISTANCE / radius. No migration to the newer AutocompleteSuggestion / Place (importLibrary('places')) classes exists.
